heading: Configure An External Identity Provider Application
body:
To configure Federated Authentication, you must have an external
SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) IdP (Identity Provider) application. In the SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) IdP (Identity Provider),
you must perform the following:

- Create a new application for Atlas.

- Configure initial SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) values for the new application:

  - Set placeholder values for the following fields:

    - **SP Entity ID or Issuer**

    - **Audience URI**

    - **Assertion Consumer Service (ACS) URL**

  - Set valid values for the following fields:

    | Field               | Value                                                                                                                                 |
    | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | Signature Algorithm | Algorithm used to encrypt the IdP (Identity Provider) signature. Atlas supports the following signature algorithm values:SHA-1SHA-256 |
    | Name ID             | Valid email address.IMPORTANT: Name ID is used as both the email address and the username of your Atlas user account.                 |
    | Name ID Format      | urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecifiedurn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress                           |

  - Create attributes with the following Attribute Names for the
    following SAML Attribute Values:

    | SAML Attribute Name | SAML Attribute Value |
    | ------------------- | -------------------- |
    | firstName           | First Name           |
    | lastName            | Last Name            |
    | memberOf            | User Groups          |

    > **Note:** The names of these attributes are case sensitive. Type these
    > attribute names as shown in
    > [camelCase](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camel_case).

  - Save these values.

Once you have completed the initial setup for your IdP (Identity Provider) application,
you link the IdP (Identity Provider) to Atlas to federate your users' logins.
